heading: Transferred
label: 14042 Transferred
notes: CodificationSection 14042 was editorially reclassified as , Crime Control and Law Enforcement.section 12431 of Title 34

Study of State Laws Regarding Insurance Discrimination Against Victims of Violence Against Women, , , directed the Attorney General to conduct a national study to identify State laws that address insurance discrimination against victims of domestic violence and sexual assault and to submit to Congress a report and recommendations based on that study not later than 1 year after .Pub. L. 106–386, div. B, title II, § 1206114 Stat. 1507Oct. 28, 2000Oct. 28, 2000

Study of Workplace Effects From Violence Against Women, , , directed the Attorney General to conduct a national survey of programs to assist employers and employees on appropriate responses in the workplace to victims of domestic violence, stalking, or sexual assault, and not later than 18 months after , to submit to Congress a report and recommendations based on that survey.Pub. L. 106–386, div. B, title II, § 1207114 Stat. 1507Oct. 28, 2000Oct. 28, 2000

Study of Unemployment Compensation for Victims of Violence Against Women, , , directed the Secretary of Labor, in consultation with the Attorney General, to conduct a national study to identify the impact of State unemployment compensation laws on victims of domestic violence when the victim’s separation from employment is a direct result of the domestic violence and to submit to Congress a report and recommendations based on that study not later than 1 year after .Pub. L. 106–386, div. B, title II, § 1208114 Stat. 1508Oct. 28, 2000Oct. 28, 2000
